ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT MUST FILE A RESPONSIVE PLEADING WITHIN 2 DAYS. Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption
Off Calendar Jul-06-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF SAN FRANCISCO FEDERAL CREDIT UNION'S NOTICE OF HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION.
Appearances: Claudia Huttner of the Law Office of Gary Fertig (510.521.1400) for plaintiff and moving party; Jesse Parmer (510.397.1270) defendant and responding party appearing in propria persona.
Discussed and the court modified its ruling as follows: claim of exemption is granted in part. The court directs the levying officer to release $1,301.47 to the judgment creditor and $1,301.46 to the judgment debtor. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR #5976

Ntc And Motion To Set Aside Default And Deault Judgment
Off Calendar Apr-27-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT JESSIE PARMER'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEAULT JUDGMENT. Appearances: Robert Sprague (no phone number provided) specially appearing for Gary Fertig, counsel for plaintiff and responding party; Jacob Barker (no phone number provided) for defendant and moving party. Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: defendant's motion to set aside default and default judgment is granted. Defendant must file a responsive pleading within 2 days. Order signed in open court. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
